data.heroHeadline: We Bridge Electrical Safety Management with Innovative Solutions in USA
data.heroSubtext: Delivering innovative power system solutions by integrating advanced technology and strategic expertise.
data.heroImageAlt: Carelabs engineering team reviewing power system studies at a US facility
data.missionBody: Carelabs exists to eliminate preventable electrical injuries in American workplaces. Every year, electrical arc flash incidents injure thousands of US workers and kill hundreds more. Most of these incidents are preventable with proper hazard analysis, appropriate PPE, and sound work practices. Our mission is to give facility operators and their workers the engineering analysis, compliance documentation, and technical knowledge they need to manage electrical hazards effectively. We measure our success not just in projects completed and reports delivered, but in the workers who go home safely because our studies helped identify and mitigate the hazards they face. That purpose drives every project we undertake, from small commercial facilities to Fortune 500 industrial complexes.

values.title: Safety First
values.description: Every decision we make — from how we scope a project to how we write a report — is evaluated against the question: does this make workers safer? Safety is not a department or a policy at Carelabs; it is the reason we exist.

values.title: Technical Excellence
values.description: Electrical safety engineering is a discipline where errors have serious consequences. We hold ourselves to the highest standards of technical accuracy, using industry-leading ETAP software, current IEEE and NFPA standards, and peer review on every project.

values.title: Client Partnership
values.description: We build long-term relationships with our clients, not transactional engagements. We invest in understanding your facility, your operations, and your compliance challenges so that our studies are actionable and our recommendations are practical.

values.title: Innovation
values.description: The power systems and electrical safety landscape is constantly evolving. We stay ahead of changes in IEEE 1584, NFPA 70E, OSHA regulations, and protective relay technology to ensure our clients always benefit from the most current engineering knowledge.

team.bio: Dr. Mitchell is a licensed Professional Engineer with 25 years of power system engineering experience. He holds a PhD in Electrical Engineering from Texas A&M University and is a member of the IEEE Power & Energy Society and the NFPA Technical Committee on Electrical Safety in the Workplace. He has led over 500 arc flash studies and power system projects across North America.
team.name: Dr. James Mitchell, PE

team.bio: Sarah Chen is a licensed Professional Engineer and Certified Fire and Explosion Investigator with 18 years of experience in electrical safety and power system analysis. She specializes in arc flash hazard mitigation and has developed electrical safety programs for clients ranging from community hospitals to Fortune 100 manufacturers. Sarah leads Carelabs' team of 15 engineers and project managers.
team.name: Sarah Chen, PE, CFEI

team.bio: Marcus Johnson is a licensed Professional Engineer specializing in protective relay coordination and power system protection with 20 years of experience. He holds certifications in multiple protective relay platforms and has extensive experience with modern digital relays, IEC 61850, and arc flash detection systems. Marcus leads Carelabs' relay coordination and protection practice.
team.name: Marcus Johnson, PE
